questions.prompt: A call drops at almost exactly 30 minutes every time. The trace shows a BYE with `Reason: SIP;cause=408;text="Session timer expired"`. What's the diagnostic question?
choices: Which codec did the parties negotiate?
Which side was supposed to send the refresh re-INVITE, and why didn't the refresh reach the other side?
Was the customer's password rotated recently?
questions.explanation: RFC 4028 session-timer expiry means the named refresher didn't send (or its refresh didn't arrive) in time. The investigation is which side was the refresher per the 200 OK's `Session-Expires` header, and whether the refresh INVITE was sent and reached the far side. A stateful middlebox dropping the refresh is the common root cause.

questions.prompt: After a customer presses hold and then resume, the SIP re-INVITE with `a=sendrecv` succeeds with 200 OK but the customer reports the far side can't hear them. RTP Streams shows the resumed direction missing. Where do you investigate?
choices: B2BUA media re-bridge behaviour on the resume re-INVITE
questions.explanation: SIP succeeded (200 OK to the resume re-INVITE) but media in one direction didn't restart. B2BUAs bridge media independently from signalling; some implementations fail to re-bridge after hold/resume and the media path stays half-closed. Capture on both sides of the suspected B2BUA confirms whether the issue is the bridge.
